title: Runtime universal fallback: when the static dispatch chain (primary + profile.cli_fallback) is fully unavailable/exhausted, DISCOVER system-available CLIs via bridge.Doctor and append policy-allowed ones to the chain instead of halting
why: Verified 2026-07-20: llmroute.candidatesFrom builds a STATIC chain = primary + profile.cli_fallback; Probe/clihealth reorder by PATH-availability + bench but never ADD an unconfigured-but-installed CLI. The 'follow policy to pick an available CLI' logic (setup.Recommend.chooseCLI = first available allowed family) runs ONLY at `evolve setup` time, writing profiles once. So a running loop whose whole chain dies (e.g. claude uninstalled, or agy-only + all agy quota-walled) HALTS rather than discovering another system CLI and continuing. Operator wants a true universal fallback: try all, and if everything configured fails, find whatever LLM CLI is on the system (bridge.Doctor already enumerates binary+auth+capability) and route per policy.
how_to_apply: Add a LAST-RESORT tier to llmroute.Probe (or a new pass after ApplyDriverBench): when every candidate in the static chain is PATH-missing or family-benched, consult a cached bridge.Doctor/DetectReport (binary_present && auth_configured && verdict!=blocked), filter by the phase's allowed_clis + policy, and APPEND those discovered drivers to the tail (deduped). Cache the Doctor probe from the preflight (already runs it) to avoid per-dispatch cost. Preserve fail-loud: if discovery ALSO finds nothing, keep the current halt. Gate behind policy (e.g. workflow.universal_fallback default on) so operators who want a hard pin can disable. Regression tests: chain-all-missing + one discovered allowed CLI -> appended & dispatched; discovered-but-not-allowed -> not appended; nothing available -> halt unchanged.
